note: code_license vs weights_license split; commercial_use & redistribution audited. Verified by adversarial license workflow wf_239c89dc-c14 (web + knowledge).

adversarial_landmines.claim: EdgeSAM weights are safe for unrestricted COMMERCIAL use in a permissive-core package
adversarial_landmines.actual_license: NTU S-Lab License 1.0 (non-commercial / research-only). Source: https://github.com/chongzhou96/EdgeSAM/blob/master/LICENSE and README https://github.com/chongzhou96/EdgeSAM/blob/master/README.md . NOT Apache-2.0.
adversarial_landmines.reasoning: REFUTED. EdgeSAM (chongzhou96/EdgeSAM, MMLab@NTU S-Lab) is released under the NTU S-Lab License 1.0, an explicitly NON-COMMERCIAL license, not Apache-2.0. The LICENSE grant reads verbatim: "Redistribution and use FOR NON-COMMERCIAL PURPOSE in source and binary forms, with or without modification, are permitted provided that the following conditions are met..." and "In the event that redistribution and/or use FOR COMMERCIAL PURPOSE in source or binary forms... is required, please contact the contributor(s) of the work." The README confirms: "This project is licensed under NTU S-Lab License 1.0." Because the grant explicitly covers "source AND BINARY forms," the restriction reaches the pretrained checkpoints/weights, not merely the code — so weights cannot be characterized as permissive. Commercial use is at best CONDITIONAL on obtaining separate written permission from the contributors; it is therefore not "unrestricted" and cannot be bundled in a permissive-core package. Secondary risk: EdgeSAM is distilled from Meta SAM on the SA-1B dataset, which itself carries research-only terms, but the S-Lab license alone already defeats the claim. NOTE A CODEBASE ERROR: /home/arash/PycharmProjects/VisionServeX/src/visionservex/model_zoo/manifest.py:1099-1116 incorrectly records license="Apache-2.0", license_risk="none", and notes "EdgeSAM is Apache-2.0." This is factually wrong and must be corrected to S-Lab License 1.0 / non-commercial before any commercial-core gating decision relies on it. No evidence was found of any Apache-2.0 relicensing or a separate permissive weights release; default-to-refuted stands and is positively corroborated.

adversarial_landmines.claim: TinySAM and Q-TinySAM published checkpoints are safe for unrestricted COMMERCIAL redistribution
adversarial_landmines.actual_license: CODE: Apache-2.0 (github.com/xinghaochen/TinySAM; Huawei Noah's Ark Lab / xinghaochen, AAAI 2025). WEIGHTS: author-applied Apache-2.0 tag on both Hugging Face model cards (xinghaochen/tinysam and xinghaochen/Q-TinySAM) and GitHub release checkpoints. HOWEVER the weights are derivative of Meta's SA-1B dataset, which is governed by the SA-1B Dataset Research License ("Research purposes only"; license type listed by Meta as "Limited") — a separate, non-commercial/research-only license that is NOT the Apache-2.0 that covers Meta's SAM model+code.
adversarial_landmines.reasoning: Code-vs-weights: The TinySAM CODE is cleanly Apache-2.0 and is commercially usable. The CHECKPOINTS are also labeled apache-2.0 by the authors on both HF model cards (huggingface.co/xinghaochen/tinysam, huggingface.co/xinghaochen/Q-TinySAM) and the GitHub releases. Taken at face value that label would permit commercial redistribution. But that face-value reading does not survive scrutiny. PROVENANCE / DATASET-DERIVED RESTRICTION: TinySAM and its quantized variant Q-TinySAM were produced by full-stage knowledge distillation with a SAM-H teacher trained on Meta's SA-1B dataset — arXiv 2312.13789 §4.1 states verbatim 'Only 1% of SA-1B dataset is used as the training data for full-stage distillation.' Meta's SA-1B dataset is distributed under the SA-1B Dataset Research License, listed on ai.meta.com/datasets/segment-anything as 'Research purposes only' with license type 'Limited; see full license language for use.' Crucially, Meta released the SAM model+code under Apache-2.0 but did NOT release the SA-1B DATASET under Apache-2.0 — the dataset is research/non-commercial only. The TinySAM authors do not own SA-1B's rights and cannot override Meta's research-only restriction by unilaterally stamping Apache-2.0 on weights they distilled from that data. Whether a research-only dataset license attaches to and taints model weights trained on it is a genuinely unsettled legal question (model-weights-as-derivative-of-training-data). Per the adversarial standard, I cannot POSITIVELY CONFIRM that unrestricted commercial redistribution is safe; the SA-1B research-only provenance is a material, unresolved restriction. Therefore the claim is REFUTED. Note: this same SA-1B taint risk applies to virtually all SAM-distilled efficient variants (MobileSAM, EdgeSAM, etc.), so TinySAM is not uniquely encumbered, but it IS encumbered. Sources: github.com/xinghaochen/TinySAM (LICENSE = Apache-2.0); huggingface.co/xinghaochen/tinysam and huggingface.co/xinghaochen/Q-TinySAM (apache-2.0 tag); arXiv 2312.13789 §4.1 (1% SA-1B training); ai.meta.com/datasets/segment-anything (SA-1B 'Research purposes only', 'Limited' license); github.com/facebookresearch/segment-anything/blob/main/LICENSE (SAM model+code Apache-2.0, separate from dataset).

adversarial_landmines.claim: SAM2/SAM2.1 model weights are Apache-2.0 and freely redistributable
adversarial_landmines.actual_license: Apache License 2.0 (weights/checkpoints). Adjacent assets differ but are not the weights: SA-V training dataset is CC BY 4.0; the sav_dataset evaluation code is BSD-3-Clause; demo fonts (Inter, Noto Color Emoji) are SIL OFL.
adversarial_landmines.reasoning: I tried to refute this and could not — primary sources positively confirm it. (1) The official repo facebookresearch/sam2 LICENSE is the verbatim "Apache License Version 2.0, January 2004" with no non-commercial or research-only carve-out (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/facebookresearch/sam2/main/LICENSE). (2) The repo README explicitly states, quote: "The SAM 2 model checkpoints, SAM 2 demo code (front-end and back-end), and SAM 2 training code are licensed under Apache 2.0" — so the WEIGHTS, not just the code, are Apache-2.0 (https://github.com/facebookresearch/sam2). (3) SAM 2.1 ships in that same repo under the same Apache-2.0 terms, and Meta's official Hugging Face mirror facebook/sam2.1-hiera-large carries the metadata tag "License: apache-2.0" (https://huggingface.co/facebook/sam2.1-hiera-large). Apache-2.0 grants a perpetual, worldwide, royalty-free, commercial-use right and explicitly permits redistribution, so "freely redistributable" holds, subject only to standard Apache-2.0 obligations (retain LICENSE/NOTICE, state modifications, no trademark grant). Precision on code-vs-weights and adjacent assets: the weights are Apache-2.0; the SA-V *dataset* is CC BY 4.0 (attribution required, still commercial-OK) but that is the dataset, not the model weights; the evaluation code under sav_dataset/ is BSD-3-Clause (also permissive); demo fonts are SIL OFL. None of these refute the claim about the weights. IMPORTANT non-conflation caveat: this verdict applies ONLY to SAM2/SAM2.1. The newer SAM 3 (facebookresearch/sam3) is NOT Apache-2.0 — it uses a custom restrictive "SAM License" (https://github.com/facebookresearch/sam3/blob/main/LICENSE) with trade-control/no-reverse-engineering/copyleft-style redistribution terms. Do not assume SAM 3 weights share SAM 2's Apache-2.0 status.

adversarial_landmines.claim: EfficientSAM weights are Apache-2.0 and commercial-safe
adversarial_landmines.actual_license: Apache-2.0 (code AND weights). Verified at source: github.com/yformer/EfficientSAM/blob/main/LICENSE = "Apache License, Version 2.0" with no non-commercial clause; mit-han-lab/efficientvit-sam HuggingFace model card weight license tag = apache-2.0; mit-han-lab/efficientvit repo = Apache-2.0. Underlying training data SA-1B is a separate research-only/non-commercial Meta Data License, but the trained weights are published under Apache-2.0.
adversarial_landmines.reasoning: I tried to refute this and could not — the weights license is positively confirmed as Apache-2.0 at primary sources, so refuted=false. Evidence: (1) yformer/EfficientSAM LICENSE file is verbatim Apache License 2.0 (fetched twice, no NC restriction); weights ship in-repo under that license. (2) The model the codebase ACTUALLY runs is EfficientViT-SAM L0 (engine sam_optional.py does `pip install efficientsam` and instantiates `efficientvit-sam-l0` from mit-han-lab/efficientvit), whose HF weights are tagged apache-2.0 and whose repo is Apache-2.0. Both candidate identities resolve to Apache-2.0, so the license conclusion is robust either way. Apache-2.0 is a permissive license that explicitly grants commercial use.\n\nTwo precise caveats an adversarial reviewer must record: (a) CODE-vs-WEIGHTS / DATASET-DERIVED: both models are fine-tuned on Meta's SA-1B dataset, whose Data License is research-only and prohibits commercial use/de-anonymization/redistribution of the dataset itself. The publishers nonetheless released the trained WEIGHTS under Apache-2.0 — the exact same posture Meta took with official SAM (Apache-2.0 model, NC dataset), which is broadly treated as commercial-safe. The theory that weights inherit the dataset's NC restriction as a derivative work is legally unsettled and is a residual risk, not the stated license. (b) PROVENANCE MISLABELING: manifest.py:1067 and models.yaml:3354 point upstream_url/official_repo at yformer/EfficientSAM (Meta), but the engine loads EfficientViT-SAM L0 (MIT-Han-Lab); models.yaml:3348 display_name even says "EfficientViT-SAM L0" while the URL says yformer. This provenance bug should be fixed, but it does not change the Apache-2.0 / commercial-safe conclusion because both upstreams are Apache-2.0.

rows.provenance: OpenCV Apache-2.0 (>=4.5); scikit-image BSD-3-Clause. Knowledge.
rows.notes: VisionServeX uses plain OpenCV Canny/Sobel + scipy morphology (no contrib StructuredEdgeDetection NC model). Commercial-safe as implemented.

rows.provenance: Dependency declared in pyproject.toml (opencv-python-headless>=4.8); OpenCV core relicensed BSD-3 -> Apache-2.0 at v4.5.0. Knowledge.

rows.provenance: OpenCV main repo (modules/imgproc), Apache-2.0 since 4.5.0. Knowledge.

CODE-vs-WEIGHTS landmine: the Apache-2.0 license covers ONLY the DINO-X-API client repo, not the model; the DINO-X checkpoint is closed/proprietary and served remotely. Commercial use is gated behind paid API quota + provider ToS -> 'conditional'. No downloadable weights -> redistribution 'no'; BYOT (bring-your-own API token).

Commercial use is metered/paid under provider ToS -> 'conditional'; nothing to mirror -> redistribution 'no'; BYOT API token.

rows.weights_license: Unspecified for checkpoints; mixed backbones (HRNet permissive; SegFormer/MiT non-commercial)
rows.provenance: XavierCHEN34/ClickSEG — MIT code base (framework follows RITM) bundling CDNet (ICCV 2021) and FocalClick (CVPR 2022). Checkpoints publicly downloadable (Google Drive), not gated. Trained on COCO+LVIS (+ SBD configs).
rows.notes: Toolbox/codebase rather than a single model. Code MIT (confirmed). Same dual weight concern as FocalClick: COCO+LVIS/SBD training-data terms plus the SegFormer/MiT backbone NVIDIA non-commercial license on the transformer variants; CDNet/HRNet variants are backbone-clean but still inherit dataset provenance. Per-checkpoint review required before commercial use/redistribution.

rows.weights_license: Unspecified for checkpoints; best variants use SegFormer/MiT backbone under NVIDIA non-commercial source-code license
rows.provenance: FocalClick (Xi Chen et al., CVPR 2022), distributed within XavierCHEN34/ClickSEG (MIT, framework derived from RITM). Checkpoints publicly downloadable (Google Drive), not gated. Trained on COCO+LVIS / SBD. Strongest models use SegFormer MiT-B0/B3 encoders.
rows.notes: Code MIT. Two weight landmines: (1) training data COCO+LVIS/SBD provenance (Flickr-terms images); (2) the high-accuracy SegFormer/MiT backbone is ImageNet-pretrained under NVIDIA's non-commercial research license, so those checkpoints likely carry an NC restriction — HRNet-18 variants avoid this. Downloadable, not user-supplied-only. Treat SegFormer-backbone checkpoints as non-commercial until cleared; HRNet variants need only dataset review.

rows.weights_license: Unspecified (no explicit checkpoint license); checkpoints trained on SBD and COCO+LVIS
rows.provenance: SamsungLabs/ritm_interactive_segmentation. Code MIT (confirmed). Checkpoints publicly downloadable (Google Drive / weights folder), NOT HF-gated, no API key. Backbones HRNet/ResNet (ImageNet-pretrained). Trained on SBD and COCO+LVIS.
rows.notes: Code is clean MIT. Commercial risk is on the WEIGHTS, not code: checkpoints trained on COCO+LVIS (images under Flickr terms; annotations CC BY 4.0) and SBD (derived from PASCAL VOC, Flickr-sourced) — no explicit commercial grant on the resulting weights. Checkpoints are downloadable (not user-supplied-only), but bundling/mirroring them commercially needs review. Recommend BYOT or legal sign-off before commercial redistribution.

rows.weights_license: Effectively non-commercial: checkpoints initialized from Facebook MAE ViT weights (CC BY-NC 4.0)
rows.provenance: uncbiag/SimpleClick (ICCV 2023). Code MIT (confirmed). README requires downloading Facebook MAE-pretrained ViT-B/L/H weights as backbone init; trained checkpoints downloadable from Google Drive (not gated). Trained on COCO+LVIS.
rows.notes: Decisive landmine: MAE pretrained ViT backbones (facebookresearch/mae) are CC BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial) — confirmed. SimpleClick checkpoints are derivatives of those NC weights, so the published weights are non-commercial regardless of the MIT code, and also carry COCO+LVIS dataset terms. Per rule 5, non-commercial => external_baseline_only. Do NOT bundle/redistribute weights commercially; use only as an external/research baseline.

rows.weights_license: unknown (blog claims Apache-2.0; HF repo returns HTTP 401 / auth-restricted — unverifiable)
rows.provenance: HF Roboflow/rf-detr-seg-2xlarge model page AND api/models endpoint consistently return HTTP 401 Unauthorized (cannot read license/gated metadata). Roboflow blog 'Nano..2XLarge all Apache 2.0' conflicts with the 401; detection-2XL is PML-1.0 (DINOv3, Roboflow account).
rows.notes: Could NOT verify: the 2xlarge seg repo returns a consistent 401 (appears private/auth-restricted or not yet publicly released at audit time), which contradicts the blog's blanket Apache-2.0 claim and parallels the PML-1.0 detection-2XL (DINOv3, account-gated). Per commercial-safe policy this is unresolved -> legal review before any bundling. If confirmed publicly Apache + ungated, upgrade to core_allowed=yes/commercial=yes; if confirmed gated, treat as gated_byot_only with redistribution_allowed=no.

rows.provenance: GitHub chongzhou96/EdgeSAM LICENSE fetched = S-Lab License 1.0; clause: 'Redistribution and use for non-commercial purpose ... permitted ... for commercial purpose ... please contact the contributor(s)'. Web-verified 2026-06.
rows.notes: PRIMARY LANDMINE. EdgeSAM (S-Lab, NTU; chongzhou96) is licensed under NTU S-Lab License 1.0, which is NON-COMMERCIAL / research-only: it permits redistribution and use ONLY for non-commercial purposes and requires contacting the authors for any commercial use. This applies to BOTH the code and the edge_sam / edge_sam_3x checkpoints. It is NOT Apache-2.0. For a commercial-safe package: treat as external baseline only; do NOT bundle/mirror the weights for commercial distribution. ACTION: VisionServeX src/visionservex/model_zoo/manifest.py currently states 'EdgeSAM is Apache-2.0.' — this is INCORRECT and must be corrected to S-Lab License 1.0 (non-commercial).

rows.weights_license: Apache-2.0 (declared) but trained on NON-COMMERCIAL data (HQSeg-44K)
rows.provenance: GitHub SysCV/sam-hq LICENSE = Apache-2.0; HQSeg-44K composition + DIS5K Terms-of-Use (non-commercial) and ThinObject-5K (CC-BY-NC-4.0) web-verified 2026-06.
rows.notes: LANDMINE (training data). Code Apache-2.0 and base SAM Apache-2.0. The HQ-output-token weights are fine-tuned on HQSeg-44K, which aggregates explicitly NON-COMMERCIAL datasets: DIS5K (research/educational-only ToU), ThinObject-5K (CC-BY-NC-4.0), plus academic saliency sets (ECSSD, MSRA-10K, DUT-OMRON). Weights are mirrorable (Apache-declared, open, not gated) but COMMERCIAL USE of the trained HQ weights is encumbered by the non-commercial training data => legal review before commercial deployment.

rows.weights_license: Apache-2.0 (declared; SAM2 base Apache-2.0) but HQ token trained on NON-COMMERCIAL HQSeg-44K
rows.provenance: SysCV/sam-hq (sam-hq2) Apache-2.0; built on Meta SAM2 (Apache-2.0 code+weights); HQSeg-44K NC training data as for hq-sam. Web-verified 2026-06.
rows.notes: LANDMINE (training data). HQ-SAM2 = HQ token on top of Meta SAM 2 (SAM2 code and checkpoints Apache-2.0). HQ components trained on HQSeg-44K (DIS5K non-commercial + ThinObject-5K CC-BY-NC-4.0). Same non-commercial training-data encumbrance as hq-sam => commercial use requires legal review. Not gated.

rows.weights_license: Apache-2.0 (declared) but HQ token trained on NON-COMMERCIAL HQSeg-44K
rows.provenance: Part of SysCV/sam-hq (Apache-2.0); Light HQ-SAM = HQ token on MobileSAM TinyViT backbone. HQSeg-44K NC training data. Web-verified 2026-06.
rows.notes: LANDMINE (training data). Lightweight HQ-SAM = HQ-output token on the MobileSAM TinyViT backbone, shipped inside SysCV/sam-hq (Apache-2.0). Backbone (MobileSAM) is commercial-safe, but the HQ token is trained on HQSeg-44K (DIS5K non-commercial + ThinObject-5K CC-BY-NC-4.0), so the trained HQ weights carry the same non-commercial encumbrance => legal review for commercial use. Not gated.

rows.provenance: Verified web: huggingface.co/facebook/sam3 (HF gate) + facebook/sam3 LICENSE file (SAM License text)
rows.notes: Access mechanism = Hugging Face gated access request: user must agree to share contact info (name, DOB, country, affiliation, job title) and be approved by Meta, then authenticate to download. Terms name = 'SAM License' (Meta custom, NOT Apache-2.0; SAM/SAM2 were Apache but SAM 3 changed to this custom license). Same license covers code + weights. Commercial use is permitted BUT subject to acceptable-use restrictions (no ITAR/military/warfare, nuclear, espionage, guns/illegal weapons, sanctioned uses) and redistribution must carry the agreement -> classed 'conditional' not 'yes'. Because weights are HF-gated, the package cannot mirror/bundle them: each user must accept the gate themselves -> redistribution 'no', BYOT (bring-your-own-gated-download).

rows.weights_license: Mixed: Apache-2.0 (default 'total' task) / NON-COMMERCIAL (high-res, tissue_types, heartchambers_highres, face, appendicular_bones)
rows.provenance: github.com/wasserth/TotalSegmentator (code Apache-2.0); README/LICENSE: results of appendicular_bones, tissue_types, heartchambers_highres and face 'may not be used commercially'; free academic license at backend.totalsegmentator.com/license-academic, commercial license via jakob.wasserthal@usb.ch. Verified web search 2026-06 + docs/license_risk_table.md (tissue stats Proprietary).
rows.notes: Nuanced: base 'total' (117 classes) model+code ARE Apache-2.0 and commercially usable. But the high-resolution and several specialized subtask models are explicitly non-commercial (license/commercial-license required), which is the audited landmine ('high-res NC'). Because the package treats this as one restricted entry covering the NC high-res/tissue/face subtasks, keep external_baseline_only and do NOT bundle those weights. A future split could route ONLY the Apache-2.0 'total' task into a medical extra after legal review; the NC subtasks must remain out. Acquiring the free academic license is a separate registration (non-commercial only), not an HF gate.

rows.provenance: github.com/ultralytics/ultralytics (AGPL-3.0); HF Ultralytics/YOLO11 model card AGPL-3.0; ultralytics.com/license. Verified web search 2026-06.
rows.notes: YOLO11 segmentation weights AGPL-3.0; commercial use only via Ultralytics Enterprise License. Same copyleft constraint as YOLOv8-seg. Exclude from commercial-safe core; external baseline only.

rows.provenance: github.com/ultralytics/ultralytics (AGPL-3.0); HF Ultralytics/YOLO26 model card AGPL-3.0; Enterprise License explicitly covers 'YOLO26, earlier and future YOLO models'. Verified web search 2026-06.
rows.notes: Newest Ultralytics generation (2025/2026), confirmed AGPL-3.0 on HF Ultralytics/YOLO26. Enterprise License required for closed-source commercial use. Treat identically to YOLOv8/YOLO11 seg: external baseline only, no bundling.

rows.provenance: github.com/ultralytics/ultralytics (AGPL-3.0); ultralytics.com/license; HF Ultralytics/YOLO* model cards all AGPL-3.0. Verified web search 2026-06.
rows.notes: All Ultralytics YOLO trained models are AGPL-3.0 by default. Commercial closed-source use requires a paid Ultralytics Enterprise License (hence 'conditional', NOT free). Under AGPL alone, integrating into a commercial package forces full source disclosure. Cannot mirror/bundle into permissive core; external baseline only.

rows.provenance: github.com/AILab-CVC/YOLO-World (LICENSE = GPL-3.0, Tencent AI Lab); also integrated in Ultralytics under AGPL-3.0. README notes business inquiries to Tencent. Verified web search 2026-06.
rows.notes: Open-vocabulary detector. Original Tencent repo is GPL-3.0 (README informally says 'supported for commercial usage' but GPL copyleft obligations still apply); Ultralytics path is AGPL-3.0. Either way strong copyleft -> commercial use only via separate licensing/source disclosure ('conditional'). Pretrained weights also trained on large open-vocab corpora (O365/GoldG/CC) raising dataset questions. Exclude from permissive core; external baseline only.

rows.provenance: github.com/Deci-AI/super-gradients (code Apache-2.0) + YOLONAS.md; pretrained weights under Deci's separate non-commercial weights license. HF discussions confirm weights not free for commercial use. Deci acquired by NVIDIA, unmaintained. Verified web search 2026-06 + tools/audit_libreyolo_hf_models.py.
rows.notes: Classic CODE-vs-WEIGHTS split: super-gradients library is Apache-2.0, but the YOLO-NAS pretrained checkpoints (AutoNAC-generated) carry Deci's restrictive non-commercial license. So even though code is permissive, the published weights forbid commercial use -> commercial_use_allowed=no and cannot be mirrored/bundled. 'libreyolo-yolonas' is the VisionServeX wrapper name. External baseline only (architecture may be retrained from scratch on commercial data, but the published weights stay out).
